name: Zinc-limitation/calprotectin sensor (Zur + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: An ingestible sensor for host nutritional immunity: the host inflammation protein calprotectin sequesters zinc, and the Zur zinc-uptake regulator reports the resulting zinc limitation. Zur gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in the probiotic E. coli Nissle 1917.

input.detects: Host-imposed zinc sequestration by calprotectin during inflammation
input.operatingRange: Zur de-represses uptake genes under zinc starvation

sensing.sensorMechanism: Zur (Fur-family) represses zinc-uptake promoters (e.g. PznuA) when zinc is replete and releases them under zinc limitation.
sensing.signalFlow: Zinc limitation (calprotectin) -> Zur releases PznuA -> transcribes an sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies a fluorescent reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence reports inflammation-driven zinc sequestration.

parts.name: Zur regulator
parts.notes: Zinc-responsive repressor; endogenous to E. coli Nissle.

parts.name: Reporter-activating sgRNA
parts.source: designed for CRISPRa upstream of a weak reporter promoter

parts.name: sfGFP reporter
parts.notes: Recoverable from stool for non-invasive readout.

performance.notes: Reports zinc limitation as a proxy for calprotectin-driven nutritional immunity; Zur and CRISPRa are validated separately and integration is a design template.

safety.biocontainment: Probiotic E. coli Nissle host; add thyA/dapA auxotrophy for gut-restricted containment.
safety.notes: Probiotic chassis with a human-safety record; research / supervised clinical use only.

buildSteps.title: Design CRISPRa sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target a weak reporter promoter; check Nissle off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: PznuA -> sgRNA (native Zur). TU2: dCas9-omega. TU3: weak promoter -> sfGFP. Low-copy vector.

buildSteps.title: Validate in vitro
buildSteps.description: Confirm response across a zinc-limitation series before any animal work.

buildSteps.title: Recover and measure
buildSteps.description: Recover cells from stool; quantify fluorescence.

provenance.source: Design template combining the native E. coli Zur zinc-limitation sensor with a dCas9 CRISPRa circuit in E. coli Nissle
partsSources: Patzer & Hantke / Zur zinc-uptake regulation (E. coli)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
